ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite meal set comprising a grain-and-salad bowl with avocado, Caesar dressing, grilled chicken, and a cookie. FOODON_00002473 (chicken salad with dressing) is used as the closest parent for the primary bowl component.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, cooked — used as closest parent for the primary protein component of this composite meal
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 composite meal code covers this specific energy bowl set. A0CKP (cooked chicken meat) is the closest defensible anchor for the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps to this specific composite fast-casual meal set.

serving.natural_language_portion: One full meal set comprising an energy bowl (approximately 420 g) with grilled chicken, avocado, Caesar dressing, romaine lettuce, and a grain base, plus one chocolate chip cookie (approximately 100 g), totalling approximately 520 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken Caesar Salad with Dressing (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite) and Avocado (raw), supplemented by USDA Branded Food data for chocolate chip cookie; values weighted and combined for composite meal estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA entry exists for this specific composite meal set (energy bowl with avocado Caesar chicken plus cookie). Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for: (1) grilled chicken breast (FDC ID 171477), (2) avocado raw (FDC ID 171705), (3) romaine lettuce (FDC ID 169247), (4) Caesar salad dressing (FDC ID 172949), (5) Parmesan cheese (FDC ID 173414), (6) croutons (FDC ID 174993), and (7) chocolate chip cookie (FDC ID 174928). Component weights estimated from typical Subway-style energy bowl portion sizes. Per-100g values derived from weighted composite of approximately 520 g total meal. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entries for Caesar salad, grilled chicken, avocado, and chocolate chip cookie
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite meal. GI estimated as weighted average of components: grilled chicken (GI ~0, negligible carbohydrate contribution), avocado (GI ~15, low carbohydrate), romaine lettuce (GI ~15), Caesar dressing (GI ~30 estimated, low carbohydrate), crouton/grain base (GI ~65, white bread analogue per Sydney GI Database), chocolate chip cookie (GI ~55 per Sydney GI Database entry for oatmeal-type cookie; standard cookie GI range 47–65). Weighted composite GI estimated at approximately 52, which falls into the 'low' gi_category. Glycemic Load per full 520g serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 76.96 g carbohydrate in 520 g serving) / 100 ≈ 40.0. This high glycemic load is primarily driven by the carbohydrate content from the grain base and the chocolate chip cookie.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from Caesar dressing, Parmesan cheese, and croutons
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that this meal set contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g (estimated 1,612 mg per full 520 g serving), which approaches or exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; requesting dressing on the side and omitting croutons can meaningfully reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~40) from the full meal set, driven by the grain base and cookie component, despite a low glycemic index for the composite.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should note that while the composite glycemic index of this meal is low (~52), the full 520g meal set has a high glycemic load (~40) due to the significant total carbohydrate content from the grain base and chocolate chip cookie, which can lead to a substantial postprandial glucose response; omitting the cookie and reducing the grain portion, or substituting lower-GI options, is strongly advisable to maintain optimal glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from Caesar dressing and Parmesan cheese; sodium load; beneficial monounsaturated fat from avocado
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the avocado component provides heart-beneficial monounsaturated fats and potassium, but the Caesar dressing and Parmesan cheese contribute saturated fat (~2.4 g per 100 g) and high sodium; choosing a lighter dressing and reducing cheese quantity is recommended to align with cardiovascular dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Energy Bowl with Avocado Caesar Chicken and Cookie Meal is a Western fast-casual composite meal set available in Singapore, associated with Subway-style and mix-and-match restaurant formats. The primary component is an energy bowl comprising grilled chicken breast, fresh avocado slices, romaine lettuce, Caesar dressing, Parmesan cheese shavings, and a grain or crouton base, assembled cold and served as a salad-style bowl. The secondary component is a chocolate chip cookie served as a dessert accompaniment. The bowl delivers a balanced macronutrient profile with approximately 13.5 g protein, 9.8 g fat, and 14.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, making it a moderate-energy meal at approximately 195 kcal per 100 g. Avocado contributes monounsaturated fats and potassium, supporting cardiovascular health, while grilled chicken provides lean protein. The Caesar dressing and Parmesan cheese add flavour but also contribute sodium and saturated fat, which are relevant considerations for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The cookie component raises the overall sugar and refined carbohydrate content of the meal set. The composite glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low category), though the full meal set carries a high glycemic load (~40) due to the grain base and cookie. This meal is suitable for active individuals seeking a protein-rich, vegetable-forward lunch or dinner option, but those with sodium sensitivity, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease should consider requesting dressing on the side and omitting the cookie.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific composite meal set exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central component data for grilled chicken, avocado, romaine lettuce, Caesar dressing, Parmesan cheese, croutons, and chocolate chip cookie, weighted by estimated portion sizes typical of a Subway-style energy bowl meal set. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component data, and glycemic load is calculated based on the full meal serving size. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. This is a composite meal set record representing a field survey data listing served by Subway - Funan and Funan - Mix & Match. The exact recipe and portion weights are not publicly disclosed by the restaurants; all nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used for this composite estimate; individual components (chicken, avocado, cookie) have negligible or unreported vitamin D in the USDA database entries consulted. 3. Trans fat is estimated at 0.1 g per 100 g from the cookie component; this is a trace level. 4. Halal status is marked true based on Subway Singapore's halal certification; the Funan - Mix & Match component's halal status is assumed but not independently verified. 5. The glycemic load per serving is reported for the full 520g meal set as 40.0, reflecting a high glycemic impact due to the total carbohydrate content from the grain base and cookie components. The gi_category remains 'low' as it is based on the composite glycemic index of 52. 6. Monounsaturated fat (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated from avocado and chicken components), polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA component data and are not independently verified for this specific restaurant product. 7. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not reported or not reliably estimable from the component sources: vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries for the primary components of this composite meal.
